metadata.title: Medical Cost Containment Report for Adult Corrections (FY 2021-22, Q2)
metadata.author: North Carolina Department of Public Safety

Medical Cost Containment Report for Adult Corrections (FY 2021-22, Q2)

...

(c) The Department of Public Safety shall report quarterly to the Joint Legislative Oversight Committee on Justice and Public Safety and the chairs of the House of Representatives and Senate Appropriations Committees on Justice and Public Safety on:
(1) The percentage of the total inmates requiring hospitalization or hospital services who receive that treatment at each hospital.
(2) through (4) Repealed by Session Laws 2016-94, s. 17C.2A, effective July 1, 2016.
(4a) The volume of scheduled and emergent services listed by hospital and, of that volume, the number of those services that are provided by contracted and noncontracted providers.
(4b) The volume of scheduled and emergent admissions listed by hospital and, of that volume, the percentage of those services that are provided by contracted and noncontracted providers.

(5) The volume of inpatient medical services provided to Medicaid-eligible inmates, the cost of treatment, the estimated savings of paying the nonfederal portion of Medicaid for the services, and the length of time between the date the claim was filed and the date the claim was paid.
(5a) The status of the implementation of the claims processing system and efforts to address the backlog of unpaid claims.

(6) The hospital utilization, including the amount paid to individual hospitals, the number of inmates served, the number of claims, and whether the hospital was a contracted or noncontracted facility.
(7) The total cost and volume for the previous fiscal quarter for emergency room visits originating from Central Prison and NCCIW Hospitals to UNC Hospitals, UNC Rex Healthcare, and WakeMed Hospital.

(8) The total payments for Medicaid and nonMedicaid eligible inmates to UNC Hospitals, UNC Rex Healthcare, and WakeMed Hospital, including the number of days between the date the claim was filed and the date the claim was paid.
(9) A list of hospitals under contract.
(10) The reimbursement rate for contracted providers. The Department shall randomly audit high-volume contracted providers to ensure adherence to billing at the contracted rate.
Reports submitted on August 1 shall include totals for the previous fiscal year for all the information requested.

The Department of Public Safety (DPS) is responsible for providing constitutionally-mandated healthcare to the offender population, which includes medical, mental health and dental care. The Health Services section provides routine outpatient care in 55 facilities across the state and inpatient care at Central Prison Healthcare Complex and North Carolina Correctional Institute for Women. DPS also provides emergent and urgent care as necessary in all facilities. External providers are consulted when the clinical presentation requires services beyond the scope of the facility.

A contracted facility has signed a contract to provide medical care at a negotiated rate above the legislative rate of 70% of billed charges or twice the Medicaid rate. A non-contracted facility agrees to charge at the legislatively-mandated rate.
Unless otherwise indicated, the data in this report is for the period of October 1, 2021 and December 31, 2021.

sections.title: (1) The percentage of total inmates requiring hospitalization or hospital services who receive that treatment at each hospital.

content.text: Attachment A provides the facility/provider, the amount paid and the volume and percentage of claims. It includes all external, contracted, and non-contracted services for the quarter.

sections.title: (4a) The volume of scheduled and emergent services listed by hospital and, of that volume, the number of those services that are provided by contracted and noncontracted providers.

content.text: During the quarter, there were 1,593 episodes that required treatment at a community hospital. Of those episodes, 544 were at contract hospitals, while 1,049 were at non-contract hospitals. Attachment B provides the volume by hospital.

sections.title: (4b) The volume of scheduled and emergent admissions listed by hospital and, of that volume, the percentage of those services that are provided by contracted and noncontracted providers.

content.text: Attachment C shows the scheduled and emergent admissions for the quarter. This includes 376 admissions from emergent services and 32 scheduled admissions. Contact hospitals provided 50.25% of the services, and non-contract hospitals provided 49.75% of the services.

sections.title: (5) The volume of inpatient medical services provided to Medicaid-eligible inmates, the cost of treatment, the estimated savings of paying the nonfederal portion of Medicaid for the services, and the length of time between the date the claim was filed and the date the claim was paid.

content.text: During the quarter, 105 cases were identified as eligible for Medicaid. Based upon the
State Auditor's May 2012 Financial Related Audit Report

, the average savings of each case would be $18,181.81. Using the methods applied in the audit, the estimated savings for the quarter would be approximately $1,909,090.05. Information regarding the length of time between the date the claim was filed and the date the claim was paid is not available to DPS. Once the application is completed, it is processed at the county level and then sent to DHHS for payment.

sections.title: (5a) The status of the implementation of the claims processing system and efforts to address the backlog of unpaid claims.

content.text: The third-party claims management vendor began processing claims for DPS on August 1, 2021. There currently is no backlog of claims, and the vendor pays all qualified bills within 30 days of submission.

sections.title: (6) The hospital utilization, including the amount paid to individual hospitals, the number of inmates served, the number of claims, and whether the hospital was a contracted or noncontracted facility.

content.text: Attachment A includes these data points.

sections.title: (7) The total cost and volume for emergency room visits originating from Central Prison and NCCIW Hospitals to UNC Hospitals, UNC Rex Healthcare, and WakeMed Hospital.

content.text: Table 1 shows the cost and volume for emergency room visits UNC Hospitals, UNC Rex Healthcare, and WakeMed Hospital.

elements.caption: Table 1. Volume and Cost by Hospital
elements.summary: This table shows the volume of claims, cost, and amount paid by facility and provider name for emergency room visits.

sections.title: (8) The total payments for Medicaid and non-Medicaid eligible inmates to UNC Hospitals, UNC Rex Healthcare, and WakeMed Hospital, including the number of days between the date the claim was filed and the date the claim was paid.

content.text: Table 2 shows the total payments to Medicaid and non-Medicaid eligible inmates, the average days for payment of claims and the total claims for the quarter.
Average Days from Submission to Payment

elements.caption: Table 2. Average Days for Payment of Claims
elements.summary: This table outlines the total payment, average days from submission to payment, and number of claims by provider.

sections.title: (9) A list of hospitals under contract.

content.text: As of December 31, 2021, DPS has contracted with 23 health care systems across the state. The systems are listed below.
Vidant - Bertie, Chowan, Duplin, Roanoke Chowan

During the quarter, additional onsite clinics began at Central Prison Healthcare Complex (CPHC) and provided services for 1,452 patients. These outpatient specialty clinics included podiatry, orthopedics, orthotics, audiology, nephrology, ophthalmology, optometry, and telemedicine. NCCIW clinics provided services for 574 patients during the same timeframe. The surgical center at CPHC performed 98 outpatient surgical procedures, and 134 MRI studies were performed in the mobile MRI unit at CPHC.

sections.title: (10) The reimbursement rate for contracted providers. The Department shall randomly audit high-volume contracted providers to ensure adherence to billing at the contracted rate.

content.text: Table 3 includes the reimbursement rate for contracted providers as of December 31, 2021. During the quarter, DPS audited 108 claims of contracted providers that were over $100,000. The audit found 89% of claims were paid according to the provider’s contracted rate. The denied claims included Medicaid-eligible offenders, duplicates, or billing errors.

Outpatient: 70% of billed or 2X Medicaid
Inpatient: 31% of billed charges
NOVANT NEW HANOVER REGIONAL MED CTR

elements.caption: Table 3. Reimbursement Rates – Audit Results
elements.summary: This table displays the reimbursement rates for various contracted providers.
